items.title: Re: Logic bug in the Linux kernel's __ptrace_may_access() function
items.desc:
Posted by Sam James on May 15Salvatore Bonaccorso writes:

To correct myself now (sorry, I was up quite a while yesterday when I
first saw reports of this bug): Qualys's reply says =2 or =3 would be
enough at least with what we know so far.

items.desc: 14-year-old girl raped in Yalal, Vikarabad; 18-year-old neighbour arrested. Police booked POCSO case and remanded him to judicial custody.

items.title: ‘Cancer Atlas of T’ presented to CM
items.desc: Cancer Atlas of Telangana presented to CM Revanth Reddy; report to guide statewide cancer screening centers, new regional hospitals, and improved Aarogyasri treatment.

items.title: Man gets life for sexually assaulting 16-year-old niece
items.desc: Hyderabad LB Nagar court sentenced a minor’s uncle to life for rape under POCSO; his brother and mother got 3 years for intimidation and forced miscarriage.

items.title: Woman, lover held for husband’s murder that was passed off as accident in Rae Bareli
items.desc: Rae Bareli police arrested a woman and her lover for allegedly strangling her husband and dumping the body roadside to stage an accident in Dalmau.

items.title: Baner-Balewadi residents to protest today against frequent power cuts

items.desc: PCMC seeks a six-month extension to complete hearings on 50,000 objections to its draft Development Plan, as the hearing panel is still not fully formed.

items.title: Disney Purchases Rights To 'Robot Jox' Franchise In Blockbuster $7 Billion Deal
items.desc: BURBANK, CA — In a surprise movie, The Walt Disney Company announced that it had acquired the rights to the 1990 film Robot Jox in a massive $7 billion deal.

items.title: 2 AC coaches of Hyd-Jaipur train catch fire; no injuries
items.desc: Fire broke out in two AC coaches of Hyderabad–Jaipur Special Express at Hyderabad Deccan station; no injuries. B1 gutted, B2 damaged; cause probed.

items.title: Trump claims 'fantastic trade deals' with China. What really happened?
items.desc: Although the much-hyped summit between the presidents of China and the U.S. ended with smiles and handshakes between the leaders of the two superpowers, the two-day meeting in Beijing appeared from the outside to be far heavier on ceremony than on specifics.
